consistency.assessment: Consistent with standard D&D 5e Wizard class features. The core mechanic (adding two new spells per level) is a fundamental part of how the Wizard class grows.
consistency.details: The feature explicitly references gaining a level and matching spell level to available spell slots, which aligns perfectly with the class progression system.

quality.assessment: High quality. The feature is clear, concise, and crucial for the class's identity.
quality.details: It clearly defines the acquisition method (free addition upon level up) and the main constraint (spell level must match available slots). The mention of finding spells via adventures provides necessary context for ongoing gameplay.

mechanics.assessment: Mechanically sound and central to the Wizard class.
mechanics.details: This is the primary method by which a Wizard expands their known spells, which is their main resource pool outside of prepared spells. The constraint 'must be of a level for which you have spell slots' ensures progression is tied to slots gained from the Wizard table, preventing early access to high-level magic.

analysis.suggested_edits: None required. The description is accurate and complete for defining this feature.
